doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_const_euler_bs_1 (mpfr/src/const_euler.c L73-L135). Binary-splitting recursion for the first of two product series used in Euler-Mascheroni constant computation. The 6-tuple {P, Q, T, C, D, V} extends the simpler 3-tuple {P, Q, T} of bs_2 with three additional accumulators (C, D, V).

Base case (n2 - n1 == 1):
  P = N^2
  Q = (n1+1)^2
  C = 1
  D = n1+1
  T = N^2  (same as P)
  V = N^2  (same as P)

Recursive case (n2 - n1 > 1):
  m = (n1 + n2) / 2
  recurse (n1, m) cont=1 -> L
  recurse (m, n2) cont=1 -> R
  if cont: P = L.P * R.P
  Q = L.Q * R.Q
  D = L.D * R.D
  T = L.P * R.T + R.Q * L.T
  if cont: C = L.C * R.D + R.C * L.D
  V = R.D * (R.Q * L.V + L.C * L.P * R.T) + L.D * L.P * R.V  -- see C source

The TS port takes (n1, n2, N, cont) as bigints and returns an object {P, Q, T, C, D, V}.

Class 'misc'. Tag minimums per Rule 7 apply.
c_signature: static void mpfr_const_euler_bs_1(mpfr_const_euler_bs_t s, unsigned long n1, unsigned long n2, unsigned long N, int cont)
divergence_from_c:
Three divergences:

  1. Visibility: C static -> TS internal.

  2. Return shape: C void with mpz_t s output struct -> TS object {P, Q, T, C, D, V}.

  3. Storage: C mpz_t -> TS bigint.

The cont flag controls whether P and C are accumulated; the recursive structure mirrors bs_2.
refs: mpfr/src/const_euler.c L73-L135 -- the C reference body.
mpfr/src/const_euler.c L138-L180 -- sister function mpfr_const_euler_bs_2 (already shipped at composite=1.0).
